review_text: Very clean and peaceful. Staff are helpful and kind.

Just travelled from perth to ningaloo and back and stayed in some very average accom. But this 1 was the worst. Moved from a hidious hotel in Bluff point to here and  its worse. Shower hammered and no pressure . No consistent temp 200.00 for this is a disgrace

Avoid this place!!
During the night, someone smashed our car window and stole valuable belongings. Apparently, this is not the first time this has happened.
We had the best relaxing stay here with our dog. Staff were very accommodating and we had room to move.

Advice from reception. "lock up everything"
Advice from first person I spoke to. "Lock up everything ,dont leave anything out."

This is a tough one - staff , facilities are first rate - nice clean en-suite caravan site , great shower bathroom. etc-nice shaded large spots - good kitchen & laundry - so why 3 stars ? Holy crap
Is it loud - all night trains running , cargo ships loading - the industrial park , rail head and ship
Yard is right next door and they run all night :$ no fault of people or park but if you’re looking for a quiet place at night then get some high grade ear plugs - but if noise is not an issue then. 5 stars all the way  ! I hate to leave a review based on things not in parks control but at the end of that day - sleep Is the goal for me

One site of the amenities are very dated and no key needed for most of the time. The ladies in the office were very friendly but thats about it.. They warned us about theft upon arrival. On our first night our car window got smashed in at 3am.. That night police didnt even bother coming out! Said they were busy and would contact us (which they never did or never showed up)...
Apart from the groundsman doing a lap in his ute that was all they really did for us..
Local car window mob told us they get regular business from the caravan park..

After the incident we looked at reviews and noticed that the park makes a lot of promises about their safety but still only have 5 cameras over the whole park. They know where they are jumping over the fence but still no action!.

Lovely place service was amazing, we breakdowns and they were really kind and helpful. They have also bus stop outside where you can go to the city center. You have walk and see the sea and even the lighthouse
High theft area. Not recommended. Cost me over $1000 to replace stolen goids.

Not too bad a location but could hear the trains shunting all night.|Apparently there is a lot of theft so lock everything up (was warned twice).|Take a torch with you to use the amenities in the middle of the night as the lights turn off automatically, leaving you sitting in total darkness.|Staff are lazy and lack social skills.|1. Did not follow up on the disgusting sewer smell (just moved sites so as not to offend the “strange” person letting out grey/black water directly onto the ground). The stench and 100’s of flies was so putrid that you couldn’t sit outside.|2. Staff too lazy to knock on the doors of two amenities blocks, or put up a couple of signs to say they were turning the water off. Hair full of shampoo, water cut off, freezing cold and NO WARNING or advice until too late. Was apparently a leak and “very urgent” but only a relatively small pool of water in a lower area of the park where nobody was camping/parked. (husband checked it out and spoke to repairer).|Do not recommend this park.
Wonderful stay again
Nice caravan park pretty clean and organised

Good communication. Quick checkin.
We had a cabin. It was clean and contained what we needed to be independent.
Pool, playground, BBQ area, inflatable pillow for kids to play on.
Close to the beach.
Short drive into Geraldton city. Can use Beam public scooters from here.

This van park has plenty of room for tents, vans, mobile homes and plenty of cabins available.
I noted a sign to ensure valuables are locked up on the way in, then the lovely receptionist mentioned it as well, I asked if theft was a big problem here & she answered from time to time.
As I was setting up camp a couple of young lads said G’day & warned me about valuables again sharing their experience of thieves coming in the early hours looking for things to steal, they once slipped their hand into a slightly open van window to grab a bag & run while the camper was sleeping in the vehicle, they have also broken windows to get what they want. This made me quite paranoid as there is not a lot of options on a motorcycle to lock things up, I did wake a number of times during the night thinking I heard something near the bike. Ultimately I had nothing stolen but it was not a pleasant feeling being paranoid about my gear.
The ammenities were clean & in good condition, the park is situated in an industrial area so could hear trains shunting all night along with reverse beepers of other mobile equipment, not too loud but I could rehear it.
房間整潔，環境安靜，價錢合理，靠近海邊及各境點。
Very nice view
Great for an excellent night’s sleep as we passed through!!!

Took our deposit and sent us a confirmation email back in January drove up from Perth for the Easter weekend to be told our 5 sites (2 ensuits and 3 powered) had been cancelled and booked to other people. They then offered us unpowered sites which we had no choice to take seen as we were so far from home and the Easter weekend. Did not offer us any compensation for their error just this is all we can do even though our booking for the sites was done first. Would never stay there again and would definitely not recommend to anyone else.

Do not stay here... large amenities block unclean and very old... got locked in toilet for 40mins... could not find any assistance as office does not open until 8.30am... staff were inside but did nothing as my husband was trying to call out to them.|another guest was robbed overnight, smashed window to get backpack wallets inside.. $60 powered site fee ...a rip off

Had a pleasant stay overall. The room was clean and comfortable, and the staff were friendly and helpful throughout. The location was convenient with easy access to nearby amenities. There are a few areas that could be improved, but overall it was a good experience

One of the better parks throughout my travels. Very friendly, welcoming staff who were available at any time. Park was kept in excellent condition, leash free dog area was a big hit. First Park I've been to with a lawn bowls area.
Amenities block and camp kitchen facilities were extremely good- easy to use- laundry facilities the same
Would definitely stay again if in Carnarvon area.
Can't say enough about the staff support here. Really stepped up when a difficult situation affected our trip. Out of the ordinary. Thanks Michelle! Rhion & Carmen

Spent a week in this caravan on an ensuite site.
The ensuite was spacious and clean, and our site was located under shady trees.
The park is well maintained and only a few minor away from a the beach and another children’s playground.
Reception was friendly and professional.
Enjoyed our time in Geraldton.

We arrived here to stay on an en-suite site and were very disappointed with what we saw - nothing like the pictures.
The noise from the adjacent port was quite incredible!
The staff were very helpful the next day and upgraded us to a cabin.

Our car was burgled that night with the driver’s side window smashed and several items stolen.

The staff were wonderful helping us, making a better cabin available for us to stay and helping us manage the security of our belongings.
Great park , good location, great staff 👍👍
Für eine Nacht OK, Strand mit viel Seegras bedeckt.

We are in a caravan
Beautiful amenities and sites. Was really windy while we were there. Not far from beach which is a nice walk. Saw dolohins there one morning. Dog friendly
Excellent park away from town centre. Staff are friendly, helpful and knowledgeable.  Would stay here again
Good facilities for family. Near the coast! There are cockroaches in room
Very relaxing weekend 😊

Stayed here for 1 night travelling from Exmouth back to Perth. Room was OK, what I expected. However, WiFi didn't work at all. Wasnt close to any restaurants either. Very easy access to beach. One of the best sunsets I saw in Oz.

We stayed at the park just after Christmas for 7 days. The park in general was nice, with a nice pool and play area for the kids, and the staff was friendly, and it is nice and close to a lovely beach area, but unfortunately our car got broken into and our ebike was stolen, as the park is not closed at the entrance and anyone can enter and exit the park as they please. We won't be visiting thr park or Geraldton again.

Thanks so much to the team at Belair! We stayed for our first time in a deluxe holiday unit and it was great. The best part was the wonderful staff who couldn't have been more helpful. We'll be back for sure!

Lovely caravan park, great location and good amenities only down fall is that there is continuous theft and break ins. We had our car window smashed for a bag that just had nappies in it that was then dumped further down, looking at reviews seems to have been going on for months and staff seem to think it’s 1 guy, but as he knows what he is doing and avoids cameras no one has been able to catch him. One silver lining is that the staff members regularly checked up on us to make sure we were okay and asked if they could help in anyway, unfortunately won’t be going back due to the safety issues. Which is a huge shame because we really liked staying here.
Cheap and clean.

Wir hatten Platz 116 für 5 Nächte. Platz war schön und schattig. Toiletten und Duschen wurden immer sehr sauber gereinigt!! Kompliment an das Reinigungsteam! Wir hörten etwas Trucklärm und auch vom Zug, war aber alles im Rahmen und störte nicht gross. Wir fühlten uns sicher in fiesem Park. Die Campingküche könnte einen Gasherd mit Ofen gebrauchen, das wäre schön gewesen.

Despite the fact that this park displays a sign on it's reception saying the park is under video surveillance, our electric bikes were stolen while locked together outside our van.  We looked around the park and could see one camera at the entry.  Of course there was no footage of the theft of our bikes. We will never stay at this park again.

Only stayed here for one night in a cabin but it was absolutely perfect for us. Clean and tidy, good location, lovely reception staff they were very knowledgeable and helpful with our stay. Wish we could’ve stayed longer, definitely will next time

We were only booked to stay here for one night and found out from our neighbours that their car window had been smashed and burgled the night before. We went on to read reviews on other sites and found out that this is an extremely common occurrence in the park. As two women travelling together we were extremely uncomfortable but mostly disappointed that staff had taken absolutely no preventative measures in informing us that there was crime in the area. It could have been as simple as a text to warn us to remove personal/valuable belongings from the front seats of the camper. We are disappointed by the lack of accountability and transparency from the park considering that theft/crime in this particular park has seemingly been an ongoing problem for years.

I usually don’t rate anything but you won’t feel safe here. Thefts are common here and the reception won’t tell you this. Police driving through park at night to check the area what makes you anxious. There are better caravan parks around you can stay in and enjoy your holidays.

The staff are hypocritical and very unpleasant. People who are not staying at the campsite and who are under the influence of various intoxicating substances are allowed to enter the campsite with the staff’s permission. Upon arrival, we were told a story that the day before there had been a car window smashed and an attempted theft.

Quite a large park within walking distance to the beach.
We had a self contained van but used the ammenities on odd occasions.
They were always clean and tidy.
Maybe a bit dated.
Being here over the holiday season it was fairly full.
Lots of shaded sites.
WiFi wasn't the best, maybe due to the numbers.
Concrete slabs for both van and annexe.
Some of the older sites only had 1 slab.
Friendly staff.
The grounds and ammenities were maintained on a daily basis.
Short drive into town.

From check in to check out, we had the best couple of weeks here. Staff were amazing, super friendly. Lots to see and do around geraldton but to come back and enjoy the cold pool, or the kids playground and bouncy pillow was awesome. Very family friendly we loved our stay.

ABSOLUTELY REVOLTING!!!!

As soon as we walked into the room, it smelled awful. The bathroom clearly hadn’t been cleaned properly — there was soap left in the sink and stains everywhere. The kitchen area was just as bad: the bin hadn’t been emptied, there were plates and forks left in the sink, and there was even stuff still left in the oven.

They basically just dumped the towels and pillowcases on the table and called it a day — it didn’t even look like the bed had been made.

Would not recommend this place at all. It was filthy.

A large park adjacent to the beach and only a short drive from the centre of town.
We stayed in a two bedroom air-conditioned villa eqiped for people with disabilities. It was comfortable, with most amenities. However, the Wifi rarely worked where we stayed and although there was a sink and detergent for washing dishes, there wasn't a dish cloth. Seals in the hand basin and sink needed replacing.
Overall, it was a comfortable place to stay.

Located adjacent to the beach and a short drive from the centre of town, this park has many accommodation options. They range from unpowered camp sites to contemporary villas.||We stayed in a two bedroom air-conditioned villa that was fitted out for people with physical disabilities. It was clean and comfortable, with a good sized fridge freezer and fully equipped kitchen. A couple of suggestions or observations we had were for the seals in the hand basin and sink drains to be replaced, for a dishcloth to be supplied and the shower mixer to be looked at.||Don't rely on the WiFi, it rarely connects. ||Overall, we would stay here again.
Fantastic location, will stay again
Great caravan park, the amenities are very clean and tidy and very helpful staff 👍
Stayed in a Very quiet unit  . Very clean and well equipped  .bed,and pillows comfortable.  Would recommend to anyone. Value for money,  beach across the road.
Facilities could do we an update, however location,pitches and staff made up for that!

A very bad experience. We were in our campervan and weren't allowed to enter our designated spot until after noon, so we expexted a clean site. When we arrived, it was full of dog droppings. We immediately went to reception to report this, and the woman there shrugged her shoulders and had an attitude of "oh really," but didn't do anything about it. We also told her that the dogs were off-leash, even though the rules state they must be on a leash. They didn't even bother to move us to another spot. The shower and toilet really need updating, and the dirty water from other showers flows into yours. Terrible campsite. Don't go there.

Firstly, I can't believe that one star review. They must have been staying somewhere else. I'm currently staying in our campervan at Belair caravan Park and it's lovely. Sheoak trees for shade, lots of space and the whole place is beautifully maintained. Staff are super friendly and helpful. This place has everything, well, almost everything. I'm giving it 4 stars only because it doesn't have wheelchair accessible toilets or showers. There is a newish shower and toilet block but unfortunately it doesn't have wheelchair access. It's a pity just one toilet and one shower cubicle wasn't made slightly larger to fit a chair. Maybe sometime in the future management could address that issue? But, apart from that, this place is a delight.

Thanks for a lovely stay Belair Gardens.
Check-in was easy and the receptionists were super helpful and customer focused - thank you, this sort of service is getting rare. We were on a site close to the amenities and laundry as requested which was appreciated.  The amenities block we were near are old but kept really clean. Our travel companions were in a cabin which was really lovely. The park is close to the beach and the magnificent lighthouse - on the walk we also saw a magnificent sea Eagle's nest with young.  It is also a short drive to Geraldton central and Geraldton's beautiful waterfront precinct.  We couldn't have wanted for much more. Thanks again.

NB Photos are of Geraldton and surrounds.
Het is allemaal erg verouderd en de sanitaire blokken kunnen een opknapbeurt en grondige cleaning gebruiken. Wifi werkt niet. Locatie is midden in industriegebied. Te vermijden!
Room needs a lot of attention. Old and dated and dirty.
Car got broken into over night and others had windows smashed. Third time in a week others told me. Otherwise great caravan park.

This campsite is really bad!! We’ve stayed in caravan parks like this before, but the price was much cheaper — around $30 a night with a powered site — and here it’s $50.

The bathrooms are separate from the showers, and some of the bathroom locks don’t even work. The hot water doesn’t work, and the kitchen is terrible — it only has hotplates for cooking.

The only good thing is that the receptionist was friendly, but everything else is bad.
The place has no charm, and you don’t feel like staying another night. It’s poorly maintained and way too expensive for what it is.

Close to town (5 minute drive) and beach across the road, downsides are it looks a bit ratty. Grounds crew could be using a string trimmer to get rid of weeds coming through pavers, around curbing and blower for built up leaves and dirt.  Amenities are very old, along with demountibles rusting etc. It's ok, just tell there's little effort that's gone into the park so if you're looking for something a little more upmarket or neat in appearance you're certain to be underwhelmed. It's more a permanent resident facility. Staff were great

We stayed 2 nights here. It gets very noisy and quite dusty with the proximity to the mine loading facilities nearby.
Also we were broken into and had a handbag stolen out of a locked car (smashed window). It’s been an expensive trip to Geraldton. There were 4 others in the caravan park who reported cars broken into on the same night.

We spoke to some people who have been in the park for 6 weeks and they said that there are break ins here every weekend.
Won’t be back.
The maintenance guy was very helpful with cleaning out glass from our car and gave us plastic and tape to cover the window.
Nice park.

Lovely simple park, clean ablutions, good powered site but vehicle got broken into. |Third time that week apparently when we reported to the police. |No warning before hand when we rang to book but the staff were as helpful as could be.

Nice place for a break.
We had an easy stay and the staff were very helpful.
Ive jumped back in to say I meant to give it a 5, not sure why a 4 was there.
We would stay again easily.

Abbiamo soggiornato al campground
Pulito, ordinato e con tanti bei servizi, compreso un ottimo wifi
Spacious rooms and clean bathroom. Well equipped kitchen.
Great caravan park .Double concrete site slabs. Lots of shade.Pet friendly. Good showers .Clean amenities.
Our 4 berth RV was wonderfully accommodated, good shower facilities and great location. Service was excellent.
Stayed one night. Perfect for during a roadtrip

Location is out of Geraldton, by the port. Were greeted with a sign to protect valuables. Later to find out there’s quite a few cars/ caravans broken into. The amenities are ancient, camp kitchen was kept very clean, and the pool was nice, but it needs major money spending on things. Also it’s quite noisy at night as it’s a working port.

Nice pool, toilets clean but dated, not too far from town, lovely beach opposite park. Noisy at night with trains going by.  Easy cycle ride into town but beware, like other reviews we had our 2 e bikes stolen in the middle of the night, they were locked to our caravan, obviously a big problem here!! Has spoilt our trip.

Dirty, dated, and generally unpleasant accomodation.

Bacon fat left in the grill, floors black with dirt, fridge broken and dirty.

Informed the reception staff who apologised, but did not check that cleaning had actually occurred and we discovered more problems subsequently.

We left before the end of our stay as it was so poor.
Great Caravan park. Staff are great. Clean amenities a bit dated, pool, jumping pillow. Huge park shady, beach a quick walk over the road. Close to town.
Schöne Häuschen ruhig gelegen

Such helpful staff. Perfectly clean unit. Fabulous location.
Our plans changed at the last minute and Michelle and her team were amazing at accommodating us. 5 stars out of 5.
Amazing service. Great location. Definitely recommend
The staff is not responsive. We cannot connect the WiFi, and ask for help. The staff said cannot do anything. Very irresponsible and not helpful to deal with any problems.
Location great,  service wonderful, bathroom facilities ..(if you like retro 1970s )just not for me. Horrendous and out dated
Never again. Worst toilet/shower block we’ve seen. Had a good size site, & check in was easy. That’s the highlight
Good spot, nice and shady and were very helpful over the phone when booking.
Stayed here for 1 night. Very spacious room. Bed was super comfortable. The night sky was
Was a great experience start to finish

The aged facilities and the regular thefts aside why would you have a caravan park next to the deafening industrial noise of trains shunting freight to Port 24 hours a day.  And how about instead of warning about theft you install a gate and some security.  Only giving one star because I cant give zero.
The reception staff were very friendly and helpful. Everything was clean and spacious and comfortable.
Nice caravan park but staff a little inflexible.

This is a good caravan park. The location is close enough to town and beach. It was windy in Geraldton when we were there but seemed to be alittle sheltered where we were. The only reason for 3.5 rating is that the ensuite sites are abit old. Saying this they are ok and I will be returning here if in Geraldton again.

Beautiful part of the world, friendly staff and excellent service. The chalet was comfortable and had everything you need. I've heard a few stories about undesirables scoping out vehicles but I didn't have any issues. Would definitely stay here again.
Very nice rooms. Clean and well presented. The showers have continuous hot water.
Lovely quiet place to stay in my RV
Easy to town, bike paths, cheap gas fills,old toilet abit smelly, across the road to swim beach, plenty of trains but not too noisy.

The caravan park section near the ancient ablutions block  is desperatly in need of renovations. The road is dirt - not gravel not paved. The dirt is carried into your caravan. I can only imagine how it is when it rains 🥺
Little grass on a grassed site, train lines on other side of brick fence if unfortunate to get the back sites. No customer service when arriving. Long way from shops.

Large cv park good easy access sites clean amenities.
Internal rds pot holy in places due to weather
Old section grassed area needs attention ...old amenities block needs a rebuild......but reception and bookings service ex ellent...

清幽環境適度假
室內空間亦整潔
唯獨網路斷音訊
與世隔絕無人知

Disgusting toilets and showers |Staff friendly and efficient |Booking in before time and we're very obliging nice roomy bays for our caravan and one otter car with rooftop tent and pull out kitchen |But let me tell you the toilets are disgustingly  old cracked walls and one basin for about 6 toilets and one urinal |Then if you want a shower pick up Ur clothes etc and go to another building adjacent and just as disgusting as the otter persons next door dirty water comes into your side so as always were high heal things with stilts maybe but there is some hooks to hang Ur clothes and a bench |Paint is pealing walls are cracked I have travelled extensively but would be one of the worst toilets I have used |Very surprised as this is gday run |It's obvious this place doesn't get a health department check over as you would close it down and it needs a rebuild or even get some bathroom dongers |Please price was reasonable and close to the beach but need to drive to town which is fine just upgrade your damed showers and toilet please

Very old & run down. One ablution block at back has been upgraded but the other at front is outdated.
A lot of permanents in this park but  most appear to keep to themselves.
Having said that,.. Park very handy to town & that is a plus for me.
Good location but the park closes facilities at 6. Apart from a small kitchen there was no where to sit other than at your camp site. It was cold at night so this wasn't acceptable.

We were in a chalet, no 31. It was lovely, very clean, comfy lounge chairs, a big plus for us. Beds lovely and comfy. The park is kept very neat and clean. Office girls couldn’t be more welcoming, and happy. Thanks one and all, we’ll be back again 🤗🤗

Difficult review to write. Put simply, upon entering the grounds we decided to leave immediately.
As holidayers in their mid 30’s, this place did not pass the vibe check.
Permanent residents staring as we passed, a paddock of dongas, grim facilities. Don’t let the positive reviews fool you.

The ladies at the desk were lovely. Thanks, but not for us.

A good caravan park to stay in, lots of sites some bigger than others, good camp kitchen and amenities, lots of cabins, ensuite sites, powered and unpowered.

Some of the roads are in desperate need of having the potholes filled in leading from reception to some camp sites and cabins, the Gibb probably has less potholes

We stayed here a few nights recently and loved it. The park is well maintained and clean. The ladies at the desk are so friendly and a special shout out to the lady and her husband (never got your name, we are so sorry) - that just got a transfer from Tom Price. She was so helpful and accommodating to us and you are so lucky to have such caring team members. We hope she can see this review.
Good location but the amenities are ancient in the front part of the park. Seriously needs to be knocked down and new ones built
Ottimo campeggio specialmente dati i servizi aggiuntivi gratuiti come la piscina anche se in inverno molto fredda e un tappeto rimbalzino molto inaspettato
Great location, a d caravan hosts were super friendly, gave us a lovely welcome msg

We had a fantastic stay at Bellair Caravan Park! ⭐️ The location is absolutely perfect, with the beach just a 3-minute walk away - our furry friend Ted loved it! 🐶 🥰|The amenities|were top-notch, and our Ensuite powered site was a great bonus 🤩|We'd definitely|recommend Bellair |Caravan park !

Nice beach. Camping sites very tight and toilet blocks old and shabby.
No recycling in this day and age!
Dryers need replacing.

We only spent one night there and reached in the evening, so the jumping pillow was deflated when we reached.

Ensuite site is clean and facilities are well maintained. My boys declared this is their favourite holiday park since it has an ensuite toilet. Haha..

We had some car trouble on our recent holiday, so had to stay in Geraldton for a night. Belair was fantastic, with easy late check in and great services. The ensuite site we stayed at was great. And the kids loved the playground. A short walk to the beach and another great playground.
開車去很方便，房間門口就可以泊車，廚房設備齊全，可以煮飯，房屋整潔舒服，雖然只住了一晚，但也很舒適。不過房間沒有風筒
The park gets easily flooded so was not pleasant. However the ensuite was clean.
Great and quiet location. Felt safe and very friendly
Very good. Slightly narrow caravan spot.

Phoned to see if they had a spot for my MH, 2 humans & 2 furry friends. Didn’t take long before the lady had a spot for us for 3 nights. She also worked her magic to find a further 2 nights. Sorry but I can’t remember her name but I certainly remember her help. Thanks from 2 P’s and no T’s. Appreciate your help.

We camped here for one night on our trip, great service at reception, lovely clean amenities, good grassy sige for our swag. It's a 5 minute drive into the town, and the sound of the highway woke us up few times through the night but otherwise a great stay.
Very friendly and helpful staff

The staff are good and friendly
Sorry, but overall the site is rundown.
We were robbed while sleeping along with other campers.
I disturbed Four masked indigenous young men while stealing from our camper.
Campers next to us were bashed and robbed, multiple campers were robbed
Couldn't stay on after that and left without a refund.
We were lucky to only loose car and house keys, which will cost hundreds to replace.
.
Place needs to have security guards patrolling at all times. Notorious for burglaries. Do not stay here unless you have someone up watching your belongings all night!
Right next to the beach and close proximity to town
Location was good. Site good size. Amenities very poor. Dated. Shower head in men's doesn't work. Blocked. Worst shower block we have come across in WA.

OMG it's like going back in a time warp.
The toilet and shower blocks are the same as what I used to use in the 70's at work.
They are in very poor condition and need rebuilding.
One hand basin and a squeeze bottle for hand lotion?
I have been to significantly better Big4 parks.

We were apprehensive about our stay based on some reviews. However we liked our stay despite some horrendous weather. The campsite was sheltered from the 50k wind and our ensuite was very convenient. Check in staff were very helpful and extremely efficient. You can walk into the city centre in about 40 minutes. The free museum was a great place to visit. There are many cafes to enjoy a meal as well. The camp kitchen is basic but has BBQ plates microwave fridge and wash up area. There was a bit of noise from the port overnight but overall it’s a quiet park. I would like to come back with better weather.
Lovely place to stay. Close to town. Very quiet and peaceful. Have every thing you need.

Rezeption und Empfang gut, einfach und freundlich.
Sanitäranlagen recht veraltet, aber solide sauber. Lage etwas ab vom Schuss, jedoch direkt am Meer. Leider auch Nähe zu Industriegebiet und Zug (kann sehr laut sein, v.a. nachts).
Insgesamt ist die Anlage passabel bis gut gepflegt.

Staff very nice. Nice big sites. Location was good
The toilets and showers are the oldest I've encountered in a very long time. They definitely need new ones.
Greatest spot, newish toilet shower blocks great play ground out site was short but ok
This caravan park is definitely in need of some renovations and upgrades. A bit over priced for the facilities that we were provided with for a powered site.
This has become the best caravan park in Geraldton. The location with paths to everything Geraldton had to offer is great. 100m walk to fishing, snorkelling and surfing spots. Sunsets on the beach

We stayed on an ensuite site.
Have travelled right around the country and this was most expensive ensuite site
We had no concrete slab or pavers, ensuite was old, dated and dirty
Staff at reception were very rude
Reception is helpful, amenities well maintained

I got put to a ensuite site and we couldn’t fit in the site so we got moved and got put to the worse spot in the caravan park where the bounce cushion was and couldn’t relax and so many kids were screaming and cars going past
Lovely place to stay clean, friendly people very helpful
Good leafy park and central position with choice of sunny sites  or shaded areas.

This is a very rough and rugged place to be where the management shows NO interest for the maintenance of the caravan park. Just for the profit only!.
We came with a  campervan. The town of Geraldton is a gem.

The Caravan Park had everything we could ask for from the bbqs, the bathrooms and the pool all and all were pretty good and well maintained and just a short walk out the front was the foreshore. One thing we noticed during the stay was the female bathrooms for where we were in the park were always locked and could always be accessed with the key you received after arriving at the Caravan Park. The issues came with the weather with many of the areas flooding, lots of pot holes popping up and more and more the longer we stayed and flooding the bathrooms. We had an issue with our zipper for our camper and had fast and friendly service to try to help us out. All and all the Caravan Park wasn’t bad just issues came with the weather effecting the Caravan park.
Nice place, clean facilities and great service! Easy check-in and friendly receptionist! We only felt unsafe at the campsite, but I guess that's the "thing" in Geraldton.

Had a campsite for the night
Everything perfect from walking in to leaving. Highly recommended to anyone who stays in Geraldton
We found it noisy
Great place to stayin campervan on powered sites.

Spacious sites for large rv's. Unfortunately, the amenities left a lot to be desired. The noise from the 24-hour working wharf kept us awake most of the night. The people opposite us were broken into  in the middle of the night. The staff had warning notices at reception and also txt messages to remind us to lock everything away.
On the upside, the beach across the road from the park was beautiful.
No photos but amazing park.Would come again and tell others.
The caravan park itself is nice enough, but the amenities are far from what I would consider as being acceptable. They really detract from the camp.
Got us in last minute, nice location and views from the campsite! Unpowered sites were on grass which was nice. Staff were super friendly and facilities were good!

Not a good experience as when it rains  the site we were on gets flooded plus lots of other sites were the same. The entrance road is full of potholes. I would not stay again unless the area we stayed in was fixed.
The caravan sites were tight. The toilets and showers where disgusting. Very old and you couldn't tell if they were clean or dirty. Reception staff where very friendly.
Great place to stay
Out of town

Be prepared.. this place is notorious for thieves.. had both my bikes stolen they were locked to my Ute.
Another person had 2 generators stolen.
I booked out early. I will never return to Geraldton or this campsite. Youths were roaming around the site with torches at night at around 2am trying all the cars etc.
This is a very regular occurrence on this site apparently. Good luck..

Apart from being surrounded by water after it rained the park is good. The caravan sites in the P section are lower than the road so when it rained the water flowed into the sites. Some of the sites were full of water the following day. The entrance road in to the park also has lots of potholes. Apart from this the Abulation block was clean and staff were helpfull

Die Duschen und Toiletten sind nicht zu empfehlen.  Es wird dort seit langer Zeit nichts mehr investiert.  Das Geld wurde lieber für die Rezeption verwendet, das sieht nach Belair aus. Nachts ist es laut durch die vielen LKW,s die in unmittelbarer Nähe den Hafen Anfahren.
Very friendly staff - thank you Michelle and great sites
Toilets ok. Quiet and I loved the landscape. Swans, ducks, and other birds. Dog friendly. Free for 24hours.
Tariff doesn't reflect the condition of facilities.  Toilets are very rundown  (but clean) and concrete pads are very cracked
規劃的很好，門禁管制的廁所衛浴，會感覺安全，環境很乾淨，營地也是很乾淨。

From calling on the road and speaking with Jody, her customer service was outstanding and I asked quite a few questions.
Had to check with our traveling crew and ring back and spoke to Michelle who was equaling as lovely, helpful and pleasant to accept our booking.
Park was clean, bathrooms and showers fantastic.
Only one slight negative with the location, close to the port so you do hear trains and trucks operating.
However the location is also close to walking tracks, beach and lighthouse. Which we went to watch sunset at one evening.
Great stay.
For the price of a power sit. Its not the best

It's a great place to stay.  Bathrooms and laundry block spotless and new.

I didn't like that I couldn't 'tap and pay' and management insisted on recording my credit card details.  That's the only negative though.

visited my 3rd time in 10 yrs time to Geraldton in a campervan journey, Belair gardens never let me down. Same high standard of maintenance n services all the decade. well done, will choose again whenever i go Geraldton.

We booked an ensuite room – what we got felt more like a heritage-listed bathroom experiment, probably dating back to a time when running water was considered a luxury. The condition: run-down with character, if we’re being polite.

The taps? So short they might as well be embedded in the wall. Washing your hands requires either acrobatics or resignation. Your only choices are: glacial cold or boiling lava, since mixer taps seem to be considered futuristic nonsense here.

Then comes the night: The entire Australian rail network appears to pass directly behind the property – horns blazing, wheels screeching, as if you’re sleeping on the tracks. Honestly, it felt less like a night’s rest and more like being part of a live art performance titled “Sleeplessness in Steel.”

The single star goes to the staff, who were friendly, though sadly unable to improve the overall experience.

Friendly and very helpful staffs. We forgot to return the toilet keys to the park we stayed a night before. They collected the keys from us and sent them back to the previous park without any charge.
Thank you very much the lovely lady.

We pre-booked (5mths in advance) a 'Deluxe unit' with modern bathroom. Belair then confirmed what we had booked, a DELUXE UNIT. On arrival we paid the balance & again the receipt was for a Deluxe unit. When we arrived at our unit & found that it was not 'Deluxe unit with modern bathroom'. I rang reception immediately & was told we were given a Deluxe unit! I told them that was not correct. Told me that they were fully booked (?)-what! Then put thru to someone else and again no positive result. I spoke with a cleaner the next day who was in one of the real Deluxe units (14-17) & she confirmed that we were not staying in a Deluxe unit. This park falsely advertises the accommodation (& charges accordingly). Didn’t even give us a cake of hand soap nor any shampoo & conditioners. There were plenty of signs about thieving & it did feel unsafe. I should have read the other reviews before booking because they confirmed having items stolen. Check out Google reviews! I have plenty of photos & videos of this revolting accommodation - water damaged doors, cockroaches, stained dining chairs & more. False advertising & charges are reprehensible by this park. Go somewhere else that you can rely on getting what you paid for.

Noisy site during the night with trains and trucks running at the port the entire night. The park itself was maintained but the ablution block needs renovation and maintenance (hand dryer didn't work). There was a burglary on our first night and someone stole all the shoes which seems to happen regularly at this camp site. Overall feedback: we'd avoid staying here next time.

Good spot. Clean and good showers. Clean toilets. Camp kitchen also clean.
Short distance to Geraldton where cafes and shops are (car ride 5mins)
Beach close by
Good, clean facilities.
Avoid if you can. Toilets/showers have not been maintained since TV was invented. Jumping pillow is missing the shade sail and it’s outside of town with nothing around

Absolutely 5 stars! We stayed three nights over Easter at Geraldton Belair Gardens Caravan Park and had an great experience from start to finish. From booking our last-minute Easter caravan trip to settling in with our little chihuahua, the team couldn’t have been more accommodating. A special shout-out to Michelle, who went above and beyond - from recommending great local coffee spots to pointing us in the right direction for fishing tackle. Her warmth, knowledge and genuine care made our stay even more memorable. Give that woman a pay rise! ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️

A lovely caravan park close to the waterfront, just across the road. Good walking paths. Just a few minutes by car into the main town.
The park itself is well maintained and good sized sites. Friendly staff and a great check in service.
A great waterfront restaurant in the main town to go have an all day breakfast is called The Dome. Good prices.
Staff was great, some parts of the park old and outdated, the back of the park is very good and well spaced out. Most on double concrete pads, some with shade.

Apon arriving our site is just perfect. The ensuite is handy as well. Plenty of amenities.
Come stay and explore. We are and enjoying it.

I found the staff good till i needed to leave early i was told a $43 fee was to be taken then getting to the office on departure he tells me all my money of a $133 .00 booking fee . Not in pressed . There only there to rip you off . The cabin was way to old shower hot water was more cold than hot all taps in the bath room were all loose shower wouldnt top running. Bed was to hard im not inpressed with this park. Be aware of early departures thats ho they make there money.

The staff is friendly and very endeavoured. Unfortunately, the park is not recommended, as the locals behave very antisocially towards tourists and their children sometimes have no respect for adults. We were terrorised by a few alleged teenagers which have targeted our camper. It's best to make a big bow around the area!
Fabulous staff very welcoming and helpful, plot was clean, well laid out and had a great little bathroom. Would definitely recommend and would certainly stay again.

It's not a bad caravan park. We stayed in the caravan part. When it's busy there is alot of caravans and people but when it's quiet, it's lovely.
It does getting noisy in the early mornings due to the trains and trucks. But once you block them out, it's good.
It's a very tidy and maintained park.
1 amenities block was rather old and small, but the newer one was a welcomed sight. Its larger and updated. There is even a basin for babies. Just no change table.
The laundry was very useful. There is both coin (which is best) and eftpos available. Depends on which one you go to.
The playground is a decent size. So is the pool (which gets very busy) and shuts at 6pm. Which is sad in summer but the beach is right across the road.

We left after 1 night due to our car being broken into in the night whilst we slept. It was too windy for us (or the dog) to hear anything. Another family also had things stolen the same night. Otherwise great little park, good pool, playground and jumping pillow. Toilet blocks and laundry would benefit from a re-vamp. Staff are pleasant, friendly and helpful. Just a shame about not feeling safe.
Lovely park. Nice location. Great rooms. Would stay here again! Friendly staff.

I recently stayed at Belair Gardens in Geraldton and I thoroughly enjoyed my stay. Unfortunately the positioning of the park is in a very industrial area and the drive-in smelt a bit but once she got there it was quite a lovely caravan park. When we showed up we were given a spot that wasn’t quite right for us, so we talked to the lovely lady at the front desk and she moved us. She was extremely accommodating! There was a few interesting things about the park, a train went by at night about every hour and honked its horn really loudly and it breaks screeched (I got used to it after the first night, but this only affected us because we were staying in tents, if you were in a room or caravan it wouldn’t have mattered) Another thing was you also had toilet key one key was for the women’s toilet and then you use the same key for a different location for the shower and same with the men’s.  overall it was a really well set up caravan park and much better than any other place I’d stayed in Geraldton before. I would recommend recommend staying here. 😁

Fist time staying here and wow what a great park, situated across from the beach, and only a 5 min drive to town centre. Park was clean, with lots of trees and lawn area, swimming pool was fantastic, good playground for the kids, amenities were clean, and front service was very good. Would definitely stay here again.

Absolutely the best staff. Managers extremely friendly and helpful. We had to get towed in, and they were especially organised for such a situation. The amenities at our end of the park were pretty sad. However we are self-contained, so it didn't bother us, but they are definitely in need of a refurbish. Great pool area.
The wifi is so bad , it is unusable. If you use Vodafone just like me, you will not get cellular reception neither.
Great location and clean amenities

Beautiful caravan park and lovely staff! Such a family/ friend welcome place and the sun sets are absolutely beautiful
Will definitely be back 😀

I was quite disappointed with this caravan park. While the bathrooms were somewhat clean, they were incredibly outdated, with parts of the interior broken and poorly maintained. Even in remote outback locations, I’ve encountered better-maintained facilities. The experience was so uncomfortable that I avoided using the bathrooms whenever possible and even skipped to shower. To make matters worse, there was not even soap provided to wash your hands.

Out of all the parks I visited along the west coast, this was the worst. I wouldn’t go back or recommend it.
I always stay here! I love the location, I love the staff and it’s clean and tidy.  Honestly don’t stay anywhere else.
Great place for a overnight stop to break the trip up to shark Bay
Superb customer service. Thanks Michelle, you're a Superstar
Our chalet was nice, but there's very limited toiletries and the WiFi has no internet access
Stupenda
Nice campsite and toilets and showers , and right across from the beach

A great caravan park . Fantastic front desk service. Very friendly. The bathrooms are one of the cleanest I have used in the 2 years on the road. . The park is well set out and sites are nice and big and lots of shade.  ..the beach is just across the road

Quiet location, neat Pages Beach. Travelling north from Perth, this was a nice stop to visit the region. About 5 min drive into town centre. Spacious airy cabin. Shared laundry, $5 per wash, accepts credit card. Dryer accepts coins. Good place for morning walk or swim, and watch sunset.

Apparently they do not allow locals to hire a powered site. Maximum stay is 80 nights but when I mentioned  I was a local who lives in a Winnebago the total fee, over 3k, was required to be paid in full up front.  Discrimination +
Spent 2 weeks here. Great time. Good large concreted sites where we were. Staff very helpful and amenities very clean
Very quiet. No problems with having anything stolen . Just locked everything.  Nice and quiet.
Not happy with our caravan spot not what we asked for
A clean park with friendly ladies on reception, clean amenities, good sized sites, some with concrete slabs, some without.
Great location on the point near beaches. Easy access to main shopping area.
Was just in town for a night, nice comfortable stay

4 of us had a 1 night stay/stop over on our way home from Monkey Mia to Perth in a 2 bedroom unit, and I have to say, this place was lovely. Our unit was super clean and neat and had everything you could possibly need. Own parking bay right near our unit. The kids loved the bouncing pillow thing and the playground. It was super windy and cold (early Oct), but couldn't feel it as we were inside, with the heater on. So warm and toasty and warmed every room in the unit. TV had all the channels too! I booked and paid through Expedia, so the process was super easy for us. 10/10!!!

Out of town close to the beach.
Clean and tidy amenities.
Staff very friendly, happy to please.

I really have enjoyed our stay at Belair Gardens. A large park with beautifully manicured green lawn, big concreted caravan sites, good clean amenities and cute white picket fences and matching units/out buildings. I loved the large beautiful casuarina trees between camp sites, providing shade, birdlife and that authentic beach feel. Conveniently located to town, shops, beaches, dog park. Plenty to see in the area and well worth a look.

Loved it here only problem is thieves,  we were told about this as soon as we arrived,   to make sure property and car is locked. Our friends car was robbed the first night but they didn't lock it and left valuables in it .
But saying that I would go back in a heart beat. 🤎
Nice place to stay..
Caravan
Great park and friendly staff. Only down is the bathrooms which need a refurbishment but they are clean.
Puh das war nichts. Sehr in die Jahre gekommen und warum jeweils die Leute mit Dachzelt den schlechtesten Platz der unpowered Campsites bekommen (die 5) verstehe ich gar nicht…

Great central location. We stayed in a powered site with our own ensuit. Plenty of room on our site. Would happily stay again. On our second stay, we did not have an ensuite. One block of amenities is great, but the other is in desperate need of repair.

Pre booked 2 night stay, September powered site, 2 adults, small van. Chosen because of the GDay Park membership. Other than the staff, who are lovely there is nothing else positive about this park. Rail and road noise from the port is continuous. Roads in the park have plenty of potholes. The site allocated had uneven pavers, a patchwork of concrete higher than the pavers so a trip hazard and soft crusher dust to the side. Dilapidated and dirty looking structures and van conversions nearby and a broken garden shed on the next site.
The amenities are old, damaged and a disgrace. Broken doors, locks, crumbling walls, mould and peeling paint throughout. The showers share drains so you get the next cubicles dirty water in yours. The power box was rusted and filthy. I am amazed that the park meets any level of compliance or that there aren’t standards required to be part of the GDay Parks group.
Certainly would not recommend it.

Do not recommend this park, customer service is awful with no respect to customers. They even hung up on the call as office hours were over. Do not recommend them at all. Try to blame booking agencies when have evidence of all correspondence. No responsibility for their own down falls. If I could give negative recommendations I would not even worth one star.
Site too shady, awful ablution blocks

Been here twice in 1 month.
Jodie look after us extremely well due to car troubles.
Staff here are very friendly and helpful .
Yes amenities are a little out of date but on par with most we have stayed in the last 8 months.
Felt staff and beaches across the road are pristine.
Highly recommend.
5 star from us .

We stayed one night with my family. We were 8 people in total, two cars and a caravan. The campsite is well equipped, several BBQ spaces, kitchen, laundry, clean toilets and showers with hot water. The reception ladies were welcoming and gave us tips for our trip. The spot we got to camp was incredible and we had a great time all together.

The main selling point of the staff was that the park has a lot of crime and you are advised to keep car and caravan locked at all times. We didn't notice any of that. It's a nice little campsite with spacious sites. The location is superb across from the beautiful white sandy beach across the road. It's ideally located with cycle paths going into town. Just don't cycle home after dark because you are not allowed to cycle back into the caravan after dark (loud oversized illegal 4x4s are ok to drive 24hours) . One of the 2 amenity blocks is a spitting image of the 1960s prison amenity block 😂. You actually need a key to get in.. but trust me you only want to get in if you really must..
오후6시까지 근무한다는것오후7시도착 열쇠를 받아야하는데 직원없음어렵게 연락되었으나 친절은  물건너갔고 잘모르는 말로금고박스에  열쇠가져가랍니다.매우 고생스럽게 입실했읍니다.내돈내고 이런대접 받아야하나 억울하기도하고~추천하고싶지 않읍니다.카라반.캠핑,객실이 혼재된 야영장분위기.깔끔한시설과 분위기를 원하신다면 다른곳을 찾아보세요.기본시설도 다소부족한편임.
All summerstar parks are great this one is on a beautiful setting and the service is great. We've been there twice now.

clean and nice place  with Good shade
stayed there several times and always stay here when i head north .

We had issues with a blocked handbadin and mouldy  pillowcase, and they were dealt with immediately .The amenities were below basic for what we paid. A tiny piece of soap each and thin small towels. No shower gel, shampoo, conditioner, tissues or hairdryer.Cheap and nasty furnishing.OK for one night, but we, foolishly, stayed for two as no refund if leaving early as we would have done.

We had a powered site with ensuite.
This park has reasonable facilities but is quite shady in places with a bit of a drainage problem. After heavy rain there was a lot of water laying around rendering some sites unusable. The Ensuite was very clean and had nice hot water.
被單發霉，廚房提供的平底鍋很好用，回柏斯還特地去Mayer買回家。
停車非常方便我們開suv 直接停在小屋門口 after hour的check in是用密碼郵箱 很方便 東西齊全 還可以煮東西

Caravan sites large and best of all pet friendly. Need to do some work on fixing all the pot holes though as for too many! A bit noisy due to port with trucks and railway very close so hard to sit outside with peace and quiet. Park is clean and easy to get to all the local attractions, facilities and services by car, walk, electric scooter or bike. Park also dog friendly which is plus.

Nice Park, shady sites and level, a little awkward manoeuvring around the park. Amenities, laundry, Camp kitchen  are old but well maintained. Cabins and playground facilities,lovely beach across the road .Very helpful and friendly staff .Town very close to service all your needs.
Was clean and comfortable little chalet

The room was nice and within a 5 minute walk from the beach. The only very minor downside was the view being restricted by power lines and the strong seaweed pong that wafted over every now and then. Staff were very friendly.

Will never stay here ever again extremely rude staff n groundstaff, unhygienic, Ant ridden rooms, staff useless, don't waste your time choose elsewhere, one of the worst experiences ever! Nice for a walk to Pages Beach but paying close to $1000 for 4 day stay in an ant ridden chalet, I think not, brought to staffs attention they didn't care less 😡😡😡
5 others will leave similar review I'd be ashamed to run a business like this
Belair Caravan Park Geraldton I will not recommend ever shameful nothing will make me or my family return here extremely disappointed

UPDATE
I can not leave another review as it will not let me, so basically in response to your reply, basically calling me a liar about the Ant problem I spoke with Danny n Ricky about this whom I thought would help, and I addressed the problem quick smart regarding the so called problem with the partners that was present, it all stemmed from walking through area of chalet we rented we though was private obviously not Thankyou for replying

Stayed in unit 27 we had a couple families walked through the between the car and the unit which was rough maybe that unit needs a under cover car port or signs to say  keep to the footpath but besides that no problem close to the beach and the foreshore the port noise was bad  everything else was good well maintained lawns and gardens rooms were good size would like to back and yet being there for 5 nights and no house keeping to ask if we needed anything as fresh towels or coffee or anything like that but again everything else was fine
We stopped one night and stayed in a cottage. It was super clean as were the grounds outside.
Unfortunately we got in late but amenities were fine and looks like a good location

The caravan park is near Point Moore and the lighthouse. It's not far from anything, really.
I can't comment on the rooms as I have my own caravan.
The sites are roomy, with most having shade. Some sites are slightly uneven, but this depends on where you choose to be in the park. There are 3 types of sites available. They are either  fully grassed or grassed with a concrete slab for the awning/annexe area or a double concrete slab.
They have ensuite sites available, which are spotless upon arrival.
The ladies in Reception will go above and beyond if you have any difficulties. I broke my foot while I was staying there, and they helped me immensely, as I'm a solo traveller. Highly recommend.
Quiet beach side park, close to central Geraldton.
Great location right on he beach, nice and open with plenty of space.
horrible campsite staff, however good reception
Not a friendly reception.

Was only a stop over and it all it ever would be didnt enjoy the area much.

Bathrooms were VERY basic and a bit to mining camp feel for us.
The location is easy to find but near a factory which can be quite noisy during early morning. The room is a little bit old.
Really great for a family relaxing holiday
Amenities new and clean. Friendly service at reception. Camp kitchen good. More dirt than grass on camp sites though. Price good too.
The Belair caravan Park is clean well equipped and the staff are super helpful and friendly
Very accommodating

This is my 2nd time staying here and will definitely be back. Staff are so lovely and welcoming. Beautiful location, clean, quiet, great parking availability. Thank you for my stay and see you next time.

We had a great stay, friendly reception and other staff.
Will remember Hayley and the others as fun people who run a good operation.
Thanks
Peter & Aggie Cartwright

We stayed in a cabin.
The good: pool was lovely, room was clean
The bad: the only frying pan sticks, there's no tray for the oven provided, and cooking was difficult.
The ugly: the master bed is in the living area. Right next to the dinner table.
Nice and close to beach and town. Lovely pool and shady trees

Loved the ocean and the pool. Just be aware of the train and road train noise. This is horrible and wakes you up all night. I would have moved, but my daughter kinda got used to it and didn't want to waste money. Would hope they warn people that they put up against the back of the park in the future. Would pass on staying here again just on this as it's not very relaxing to be woken up 2 to 5 times a night and interrupted 4 to 8 times a day. See video  judge for yourself

Stay total of 7 nights. Facilitys are clean and large showers with hot water. Lots of trees for shade. What ruined our stay was the trains and trucks most of the night, as we were against the back wall and had the full effect. I would suggest not using these site. Or at least some type of warning. Would not use again, unless placed more to the front.
Got used to the train noises after the first night.

Good grassed sites with power. Amenities were well presented and cleaned regularly.
Can be noisy with the rail yards nearby.
Staff were friendly and professional.
Basic but everything you need. Going to be my new go to.
Great place to stay in Geraldton. Quiet and close to everything.

Got the powered campground. Check in was easy. I love the individual toilet/showering and it was very clean and well maintained. Maybe the lights can be brighter.

Location was pretty far off to the other side of Geraldton and the beach was a letdown. But will still recommend for a good stay!

Great park, very friendly staff. Very dated amenities but clean. Small beach across the road. A bit of noise from the port with trucks and things moving around,  nice swimming pool. Short walk to town

This park was ok. The amenities are the worst i have seen in 5yrs of travelling. They mainly put people out the front and nowhere near the use of camp kitchen. Dump point right at the back of park, and decided to pull the laundry apart on a beautifully sunning day, knowing the follow days was going to be rainy. We were mot even notified. Great location as across the road is a lo ely beach

Wir hatten einen Bungalow. Sehr in dir Jahre gekommen. Badezimmer mit Duschvorhang, küche eher schmuddelig. Die Klimaanlage hatte einen unangenehmen Geruch. Mitarbeiter sehr freundlich. Mit dem Auto kommt mal schnell überall hin. Für einen Stop Richtung Norden ok, da gute Einkaufsmöglichkeiten in Geraldton vorhanden bevors es weitet geht, länger würde ich hieg nicht bleiben.
Sehr gute Cabins mit viel Ruhe und ideal für auf der Durchreise
Clean, neat and tidy
Staff were so friendly and helpful. Deluxe cabin was spacious and tidy  with everything you need for your stay.

We have stayed a few times now. Still our go to accommodation when we are in the big smoke. Lovely helpful staff and beautiful rooms. Excellent facilities for the kiddies to keep them entertained. Plus the beach right across the road.. what else could you ask for.
I have Caravaned from NQ Queensland to WA 3 times, and have been too many Caravan Sites and I must say this is in the top 10.
Room was nice. Bit dated maybe. Kitchen doesn't seem to get cleaned. Bed was good. Bathroom very old, not great but did the trick. Recreational facilities were nice. WiFi was spotty.
It is a very friendly, clean and great place to stay. Awesome service. We had a caravan site that was quiet and close to amenities! Big level block, so work a treat. Will definitely be back!
Nice quite location, but the place could do with a little revamp

My husband and I spent 2 nights over Xmas in one of the cottages. We couldn't fault  it. It was spotlessly clean and had everything we needed for a comfortable stay. The toilet had a safety bar and the shower had a mat and a chair. Important for us older folk but not many places provide them. Very reasonably priced as well. Highly recommend.
Good laid back caravan park facilitys were good
Ruhiger Caravan Park mit Pool und Trampolin verschiedene Sites Rasen, Sand oder Beton ganz wie gewünscht. Recht zentral gelegen mit dem Auto in 5 min in der City.
A wonderful place to stay and relax 😀
Love the location in point moore , opposite the beach, in the west end

A great pet friendly, quiet park on the Peninsula right across the road from an awesome beach and playground. The beach to the left is an off leash area with lots of room to run and swim. The amenities are very old but clean. There is also a playground in the park as well as a swimming pool. The grounds are very neat and tidy. It's a short drive into town for all your grocery needs as well as lots of eateries and cafes right on the foreshore which is beautifully laid out with covered table and seat areas as well as BBQ'S.
We only stayed one night in a budget unit but it had all we needed in terms of facilities. The pool was great after a hot day
Der Camping ist draußen beim Leuchtturm, zu Fuß in die Stadt ist nicht zu empfehlen.  Der Platz aber sehr schön, war sehr windig
Great staff, bit expensive but running a park wouldn't be cheap

We stayed as we had already paid but we couldn't wait to leave after 2 nights. Stayed in powered site 49, & the whole area had a general smell of urine. There are 2 toilet/shower blocks - the closest to our pitch are extremely old, with tiny shower cubicles - whilst showering it started flooding and smelling like faeces. Toilets & showers are also separate in this block. Newer block is much nicer & would recommend to use this instead, toilets and showers in the same block. Amenities require a key to get into which can be quite annoying as forgot the key a couple of times (would be easier if they had pinpad locks like most other places). Camp kitchen is small with very basic facilities (two double electric hobs), although some pots, pans & utensils provided which is helpful as well as fridge/freezer. Closer bbq spot has basic fridge/freezer, kettle, microwave and 2 bbqs but no hobs. Quite noisy with nearby train/port.

Staff were friendly on arrival and no issues with them at all. The main problem is the area has a bad smell and there seems to be a general sewage system problem, as well as the very basic cooking/kitchen facilities.

The park is directly opposite Pages Beach which is quite nice and we saw a beautiful sunset there one night.

Laundry here is $4 per load, and in the newer laundry block you can pay by contactless card which I think is great. Plenty of washing line room to dry clothes, but dryers also available.

I personally wouldn’t stay here again because of the smell, the kitchen and location isn’t great if you wanted to walk to town (would take around 45 minutes, but perhaps you might be someone who would enjoy a walk!).

Sorry to leave a bad review, I’m normally not one to be so picky but I didn’t enjoy the stay here and wanted to be honest. I have provided pictures also to give a visual overview of my comments made. We stayed in a rooftop tent, I cannot comment on whether a stay in a caravan or one of their cabins would be more comfortable.

We had a great 4 night stay at Belair Caravan Park. It was a quiet & very well maintained Park. The staff were very helpful no matter how small the enquiry, Eleanor, in the front office in particular was great! We’ll definitely be back 👍

Only stayed for 1 night, so I cannot really say much about the place, but our experience was pretty positive. The caravan park is well laid out, clean and quiet within easy walking distance to the beach. Our cabin was comfortable with everything working properly.
Dirty and overpriced. Windows so filthy u couldn't see through them. I'd booked months ago and didn't get the correct bungalow that was advertised.

Stayed 1 night powered site on return to Perth - served our needs. Close to beach walks and lighthouse. A bit of walk to get back to shops, cafes through port area - we stayed on site. Pool was clean and kid friendly. Staff friendly and helpful.
Awesome location for our family holiday the kids wanted to stay longer. Friendly helpful staff chalet unit was very large and clean and met all our needs.
Very helpful staff. Great location. Nice beach across the road, pool, playground for kids. Highly recommend this caravan park.
The cabins were very comfortable and had a great view. Across the road from the beach and close to town. Great kids play area too.
Nice caravan park

Love the idea of ensuite camping site! Great for ppl traveling in a car not a van! Privacy, security & a place to put your things so you’re not juggling items in your car! Only downside to this spot was a bit of shipyard noise for a short period.
Excellent caravan park and ideal location especially with dogs (dog beach over the road)
Facilities block very clean,nice large grassy pitch
Great spot near the Port & lighthouse, close to town. Good, clean amenities, we had a good spot, close to camp kitchen. Would definitely recommend.
Very well maintained park, excellent amenities and nice pool. Close to quiet beach but need to drive to town of couple of kms
Clean tidy room. Very close to the beach. Playground etc for kids if you have them. No complaints.
NO PET FRIENDLY AS ADVERTISED.

It's very noisy at night. The train horns every time it goes past nearby, the road trucks exhaust brake going on throughout the night and a forklift or some machine reverse beeping from 4 am. It's hard to sleep.
But a beautiful place to stay, close to the beach and there is a swimming pool as an alternative 😀 .
A regular stop for us, nothing in the area compares.
Original comment re air traffic noise deleted as I had parks mixed up. Geraldton Park is one of the best on our around Oz trip.

Great caravan park.  Close to beach. Not far from town centre.
We didn't find the train noise a problem.
Amenities were great, clean.  Showers good pressure.

The cabinet is so poor old and smelly and everything there make you feel not relaxed i asked them if i can do check out and get refund of my money as my family didn’t really want to stay there then they said no we don’t refund we have 72 hours policy anyway I’ve ended up paying them and went somewhere else to stay there for 2 nights wich is mantra hotel. So I ended up paying for 2 hotels at the same time because of that poor hotel never again
Craig was extremely helpful with an electrical problem with our van. He spent over an hour trying to solve the problem, but in the end we had to get to an electrician next day. Thankyou Craig!
Great caravan park, friendly staff and helpful. Very clean and close to everything.

Great spot to spend a few days, or more.
Good facilities, lots of space. Not too busy in early Sept. Reasonable rates. Close to the water. Bit of a hike to the shops though.
We are in a motorhome, but the cabins look pretty good too.

On arriving and enterrig the staff to inform i wanted to check in the staff member looked at her watch to see if it was after 11am. I informed the staff i had an anxious dog and were there trees on our site with seeds/nuts. Both staff members couldnt answer me and both said they didnt think so. On parking on our site there were trees with seeds/nuts down both sides and along the back, please do you staff know anything about your park. Secondly on stating i had a dog that was anxious to noise as described above with the nuts/seeds they failed to inform me of the trains and yes we were allocated a site right on the train line at the back, and not once was this mentioned. We had discovered on our visit that we had problems with our battieries and spent a full day trying to find someone who could help. We found Battery Mart who were wonderful, not charging us to do a load test on our batteries, but advised they couldnt write a report or for us to pick up the batteries till after 9am. So, i rang the Belair office and spoke to Vicki, who has absolutley not customer service. I asked if we could delay our departure for 30mins or so, but we would be out before 11am, as we needed to pick up our batteries and install them back in our van. She stated "NO", as someone is due to come in after us, but i stated as your website arrivals not till 11am, and she said what if they come early. I wasnt asking for after 11am just enough time to connect my batteries and move on, it could of been that we would be out by 10am, or maybe 1030am. She just kept refusing and when I stated i would them on the side of the road, she said fine. we have been travelling Aussie for 7 months and only use Gday parks, but this has left a sour taste in my mouth and such really bad customer service. And yes VICKI i think you need to look at your job description AGAIN. Bad
Close to beaches & the Port. All amenities and services close by.
Nice big shady concrete level caravan site. Modern clean amenities . Close to the beach and good walks. Friendly staff. Stayed here abt 7 times, will be back again next year.
Clean, easy to check in
We enjoyed our experience praise God

Behoorlijk verouderd allemaal hier! Het sanitair kan wel een opknapbeurt gebruiken, de game room is erg saai en de receptie 'vergat' even te vermelden dat het internet het al tijden niet doet terwijl je wel een WiFi code krijgt. Maar los van de wandelafstand naar het strand is de ligging ook niet best, want het lijkt wel of je op een industrieterrein verblijft. Dag en nacht hoor je treinen en roadtrains voorbij komen. Lekker dan....😬😬

Easy to book, lovely staff and easy to access amenities. One night we were there staff were making and selling delicious meat and gravy rolls. We were camping at an unpowered site. The downside was that our site backed onto a train line which woke us up throughout the night.
Large cvan pk. Close to town and beach. Toilets could use a freshen up. Not very good for such a large park.  Reception staff were very helpful.

地點靠近工業區，算比較偏僻。

訂房後，會要求你澳洲電話號碼，車子車號跟以電話提供信用卡資訊！
對於人不在澳洲或訊號不好的地方，你會感到有壓力。

這次旅行，就這間需要先以電話提供車號或信用卡資訊，其他都是到櫃檯時才提供相關資訊。
這造成了許多的困擾。

房間還不錯，可以自己烹煮餐點。
附近有些是紅泥土地。
Based on how they treated us and people i saw from other countries, i would say the receptionists are racist and very disrespectful, don come here
Very friendly staff and great help with directions
We had a distressing situation which the staff worked hard all day to fix. Never had that much support from a caravan park before and we have been travelling for years.
Great spot, close to everything, great play ground for the kids and clean units. We will definitely stay here again!
Very happy with service, location and amenities, good security as well.
Amenities need a major overhaul.

Parked the motorhome here for a couple of days. Lovely protected beach across the road, great views along the beach if you want to walk. Tidy park with good clean facilities.
I'll be back again 😁👍
Really loved this this  Park.  Close  to the beach. Not far to town. Amenities Old but clean. Park was neat and tidy. Extended our stay which was good. Would definitely recommend.
Very noisy park, very close to industry with trains and road trains throughout the night.
Caretakers are dogs
We stay in our caravan.  It's a good caravan park.  2 amenitie blocks, 1 new and the other, although very clean, is quite old.  Good laundries with plenty of hanging space.
A nice personal touch in the on- sweet power cabin's
So good clean quiet loved it
Nice place and location, just needs some TLC to the buildings and facilities

Very nice park with good facilities.  I had trouble sleeping due to the noise which is no one's fault, just the location of the park.
Good value, bus service to town, plenty to see in Geraldton with very informative and friendly ladies at the information centre and arts centre in town.

Stayed a night in the on-site chalet, it was comfortable and quite spacious as far chalets go. Chalet was clean and well appointed. Manager was absolutely brilliant and communication was excellent. Thank you for a great experience will be staying once again when in Geraldton next.

Can be noisy being close to the port.
Amenities clean but way beyond par.

Park is lovely and spacious, it certainly meets that benchmark for the “G’day Parks”.
The amenities are clean and well maintained and centralized for easy access.
They have the bouncy pillow and nice playground for the kids, great for keeping them quickly entertained while setting up van/tent/camper or cooking grub.
One star lost only because of the proximity to the freight train line that runs all night and makes sleeping a bit interesting, suggestion; bring earplugs.
Otherwise, would be very happy to stay here again. Cheers!
Nice and quiet
Lovely friendly park ensuite bathroom nice town
